title: PeerTube
description: Free, federated (ActivityPub) video hosting and live-streaming platform - a self-hosted, decentralized alternative to YouTube.
installation: This template runs the PRIMARY PeerTube server container ONLY (chocobozzz/peertube, a Node.js app listening on HTTP port 9000). REQUIRED before first boot: 1) Rotate PEERTUBE_SECRET - generate with 'openssl rand -hex 32' and replace the placeholder; this signs sessions/JWT and CANNOT be left at the default. 2) Set PEERTUBE_WEBSERVER_HOSTNAME to your real public host BEFORE going live - it is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and federated video URLs and cannot be changed cleanly once you have federated. 3) Note the default DB suffix is empty here (PEERTUBE_DB_SUFFIX="") so the add-on database 'peertube' is used as-is; the upstream compose default appends '_prod'. 4) After first start, find the auto-generated root admin password in the logs (search for 'Username: root') or set it with 'NODE_CONFIG_DIR=/config NODE_ENV=production npm run reset-password -- -u root' inside the pod. COMPANION/EXTERNAL SERVICES (NOT bundled): (a) EMAIL - PeerTube needs an SMTP server for account verification, password reset and notifications. Deploy a separate mail relay (e.g. the official 'mwader/postfix-relay' image) and point PEERTUBE_SMTP_HOSTNAME/PEERTUBE_SMTP_PORT at it, or set PEERTUBE_SMTP_* to an external provider; mail is disabled until configured. (b) OBJECT STORAGE (recommended at scale) - videos, thumbnails and HLS playlists can grow huge; for production set PEERTUBE_OBJECT_STORAGE_ENABLED=true plus the PEERTUBE_OBJECT_STORAGE_* S3 vars (endpoint, region, bucket, access/secret keys) against an external S3 bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) instead of the local /data volume. (c) Transcoding, federation queues and scheduled jobs all run INSIDE this single container (handled by internal Bull/Redis job queues), so no separate worker/scheduler pod is required - but transcoding is CPU/RAM heavy, so give this pod a large pod size and persistent /data. Rotate the Postgres password; Valkey/Redis is password-less in-cluster (PEERTUBE_REDIS_AUTH is left blank). The /data volume must be persistent (and RWX if you ever scale replicas) because uploaded/transcoded videos live there until moved to object storage.
